title: Today's Tech Brief: AI Films and GOP Attacks
summary: A look at AI-generated films and GOP attacks on James Talarico
imageAlt: Image from source article for The GOP’s Attacks on James Talarico Are Straight Out of the Incel Handbook
imageCaption: The GOP’s Attacks on James Talarico Are Straight Out of the Incel Handbook
imageSourceTitle: The GOP’s Attacks on James Talarico Are Straight Out of the Incel Handbook

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got a few interesting stories for you today. First, a film made with AI is debuting at Tribeca. Yes, you heard that right - a film made with artificial intelligence. And in other news, the GOP's attacks on James Talarico have some interesting parallels to online communities. Oh, and a big win for Ken Paxton in Texas - that's got some implications too.

A $2,000 AI-generated film called 'Dreams of Violets' will make its debut at Tribeca. The film tells the story of the Iranian government's mass killing of civilians. What's remarkable here is that it was made with a relatively small budget, and AI tools. I mean, think about it - just a couple thousand bucks and some clever coding. This could be a game-changer for independent filmmakers, and raises questions about the future of film production. Can you imagine what you could do with a bigger budget?

The GOP's attacks on James Talarico have been criticized for being personal and unfounded. According to an article in Wired, these attacks bear a striking resemblance to the rhetoric found in online incel communities. It's a concerning trend, and one that highlights the blurred lines between politics and online discourse. When did politics get so... nasty?

The GOP’s Attacks on James Talarico Are Straight Out of the Incel Handbook is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: On Tuesday, with Donald Trump’s endorsement and the backing of the MAGA faithful, scandal-ridden Texas attorney general Ken Paxton defeated incumbent US senator John Cornyn in a runoff primary to claim the Republican nomination for that seat. He then quickly set about painting his general-election opponent, Democratic Texas state representative James Talari... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

A $2,000 AI-generated film will make its debut at Tribeca, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
